title: cyperus saturatus
side_effects: Cyperus saturatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high saponin content. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to liver damage and kidney strain, as the toxic compounds interfere with normal organ function. In some cases, excessive intake of Cyperus saturatus may cause respiratory issues, including coughing and difficulty breathing, especially in individuals with pre-existing lung conditions. Additionally, the plant's toxic compounds may interact with certain medications, exacerbating their side effects or reducing their efficacy.
